reforms.description: The federal law on universities is being revised to enhance efficiency and promote a more labour-market-oriented output, with federal subsidies tied to the number of diplomas delivered. Additionally, reforms have been introduced to create a pathway for students in the dual system to achieve a level equivalent to university studies, including the establishment of new technical institutions called Colleges of Higher Education (HES). Furthermore, the Federal Council proposed a series of special measures to promote job-related and general continuing education, known as the continuing education offensive, which includes a budget allocation for vocational and university-level training and funding for specialized units at universities to provide a range of continuing education courses.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: These reforms aim to enhance the skills of the workforce, thereby increasing productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The reforms have been successfully implemented and are in effect.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reforms represent a significant overhaul of the university system and continuing education, aiming to align education with labor market needs.
reforms.source_quote: In 1989, the Federal Council proposed to Parliament a series of special measures, dubbed continuing education offensive, to promote job-related as well as more general continuing education.

reforms.description: The BENEFRI framework convention promotes regional cooperation among universities, allowing students to attend courses and sit examinations across multiple institutions, thereby enhancing knowledge transfer and collaboration.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This framework facilitates greater collaboration and resource sharing among institutions, which can lead to improved educational outcomes and innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The framework has been successfully established and is operational.
reforms.importance_rationale: While significant, the framework primarily enhances existing structures rather than introducing a major overhaul.
reforms.source_quote: The most salient feature of this co-ordination has been the so-called BENEFRI framework convention signed in 1993.

reforms.description: The Confederation allocated SF 561 million to a fiscal stimulation programme aimed at encouraging cantons and communes to expedite maintenance expenditures on public infrastructure, which is expected to raise economic growth. This includes subsidies for private building renovations to improve energy efficiency.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The investment in infrastructure is expected to stimulate economic activity and growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The programme has been successfully implemented and is in effect.
reforms.importance_rationale: The allocation of funds represents a significant investment in public infrastructure, though it is not a complete overhaul of the funding system.
reforms.source_quote: The programme focuses on bringing forward maintenance expenditures on public infrastructure... including the non-application of the 2 per cent blockage of credits to some of the Confederation’s investment expenditures.
